name: Carl
observedShift: They are being pulled into two different directions. AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI

focusPoints.label: Choosing between AZPocket and CoE UX/UI priorities.
focusPoints.reason: Notes say Carl is being pulled into two different directions. One possibility is competing priorities across AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI workstream—worth clarifying what he’s saying yes to, what’s slipping, and what tradeoff he wants to make.

focusPoints.label: What’s stuck between the two workstreams.
focusPoints.reason: Notes imply a split focus; could be dependencies or unclear decision ownership between AZPocket App and the CoE UX/UI. Worth identifying the specific dependency, decision, or coordination friction that keeps him from moving both forward cleanly.

focusPoints.label: What he wants more of from you.
focusPoints.reason: Whether he needs air cover, faster decisions, or clearer prioritization from you so he can stop context-switching and protect time for the work that matters most right now.

focusPoints.label: How he’s keeping stakeholders in sync.
focusPoints.reason: At UX Lead in a bi-weekly check-in, stakeholder alignment is the default risk reducer: making sure both the AZPocket App stakeholders and the CoE UX/UI stakeholders understand timelines, tradeoffs, and what’s changing—so surprises don’t accumulate between meetings.
